items.title: Automate API Usage Cleanup in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new CronJob that prunes old `app_apiusage` records daily, ensuring the usage log remains manageable. The job deletes entries older than 90 days in small batches, preventing the database from filling up and improving overall performance. Key files added include a cleanup script and the CronJob configuration.

items.title: Enhance Auto Recharge Functionality in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an automatic recharge orchestration system that monitors application balances and triggers top-ups when necessary. Key features include a robust eligibility check, a new CronJob for regular execution, and safeguards against double charges and dead cards. The implementation ensures seamless integration with Stripe for off-session payments, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Trimmed DEBUG Logs for Cleaner Gateway Performance in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update removes non-essential DEBUG log lines from the gateway's utility files, significantly reducing log volume by approximately 36%. Key changes include the removal of redundant QuerySet representations and sensitive data exposure, which enhances performance and security. The overall impact leads to lower storage and indexing costs while maintaining essential logging for troubleshooting.

items.title: Introducing AutoRechargeConfig Model and Management API in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the AutoRechargeConfig model along with a user-facing management API for the Stripe auto top-up feature. Key changes include the ability to manage recharge configurations, set thresholds, and handle saved card references securely. The new API endpoints allow users to create, retrieve, update, and disable their auto-recharge settings efficiently.

items.title: Enhance Stripe Integration with Off-Session Top-Up Features in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new endpoints for off-session card setup and charging, enabling users to save a card for automatic top-ups without being present. Key additions include the creation of SetupIntents and the ability to charge saved cards, enhancing the payment experience. The implementation ensures idempotency and proper error handling for declined transactions.

items.title: Streamline Uploads to Hong Kong Bucket in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update migrates uploads from the Beijing COS bucket to a new Hong Kong bucket, significantly reducing costs associated with cross-border data transfer. The change affects 14 workers and ensures that uploads are processed locally, eliminating the need for global acceleration. All existing URLs remain functional, and the environment variable for acceleration is retained for emergency use.

items.title: Enhance Documentation with 8 New OpenAI Client Guides in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces eight detailed integration guides for OpenAI-compatible clients, including Cherry Studio, ChatBox, and LobeChat. Each guide provides comprehensive instructions on setup, API token acquisition, and unique features, enhancing user experience and accessibility. The addition of these resources aims to streamline the integration process for developers using AceDataCloud.

items.title: Scaled Down MCP Sora Replicas to Zero in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: The production deployment for MCP Sora has been updated to set the replicas from 1 to 0. This change reflects the decision to fully offline Sora workloads, effectively scaling down the service. The modification was made in the deployment configuration file.

items.title: Scaled down all Sora deployments to zero in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: All production Sora deployments in the PlatformService have been scaled down to zero replicas. This change affects the openaihk, mountsea, cqtai, wcnb, and ttapi deployments, effectively stopping all Sora workloads. The adjustment is part of the effort to fully offline the Sora instances.

items.title: Optimized Production Requests for Mountsea-Sora in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the CPU and memory requests for the platform-service-mountsea-sora deployment, adjusting CPU from 50m to 40m and memory from 300Mi to 240Mi. These changes reflect observed usage patterns, allowing for more efficient resource allocation while maintaining performance limits.

items.title: Optimized Production Requests in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update fine-tunes the production requests for the openaihk-sora service by reducing CPU from 50m to 40m and memory from 300Mi to 240Mi. The adjustments are based on observed usage, allowing for a more efficient allocation of resources while maintaining current operational limits.

items.title: Optimized Production Requests for OpenAIHK Nanobanana in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the CPU and memory requests for the OpenAIHK Nanobanana deployment, adjusting CPU from 50m to 40m and memory from 300Mi to 240Mi. These changes are based on observed usage, allowing for more efficient resource allocation while maintaining performance limits.

items.title: Optimized Production Resource Requests in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update fine-tunes the production resource requests for the platform-service-openaihk-hailuo. The CPU request has been reduced from 50m to 40m and memory from 300Mi to 240Mi, reflecting observed usage patterns. These changes help to lower reserved capacity while maintaining current operational limits.

items.title: Optimize Production Resource Requests in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update fine-tunes the production requests for the openaihk-flux service by reducing CPU from 50m to 40m and memory from 300Mi to 240Mi. The adjustments are based on observed usage patterns, allowing for better resource allocation without changing the limits. This change aims to reclaim reserved capacity effectively.

items.title: Reduce CPU and memory requests for Suno production in Platform Service ⚡
items.summary: Adjusted the CPU and memory requests for the OpenAIHK Suno production deployment. CPU requests were lowered from 200m to 160m and memory from 400Mi to 320Mi. This change reclaims unused capacity while keeping current limits intact. No functional changes to the service were made.

items.title: Optimize Memory Requests for OpenAI Deployment in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the memory request for the openaihk-openai production deployment from 400Mi to 320Mi, while keeping the CPU request unchanged at 20m. The adjustment is based on observed memory usage, allowing for better resource management without affecting performance.

items.title: Reduce web pod resource requests in X402 Guard ⚡
items.summary: Adjusted x402guard-web production pod requests to better match observed usage. CPU requests were lowered from 50m to 40m, and memory requests from 64Mi to 48Mi. Limits remain unchanged, allowing the cluster to reclaim some reserved capacity without impacting performance.

items.title: Optimized API Resource Requests in Web Extrator 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the production resource requests for the webextrator-api, lowering CPU from 50m to 40m and memory from 256Mi to 192Mi. The adjustments reflect current usage patterns, ensuring efficient resource allocation while maintaining performance limits.

items.title: Optimize Production Resource Requests in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the production resource requests for the platform-publisher, lowering CPU from 200m to 150m and memory from 1Gi to 768Mi. These adjustments are made to better align with actual usage, which has been significantly lower, while maintaining existing limits for burst capacity. This change aims to reclaim reserved resources efficiently.

items.title: Boost production resource requests for platform-gateway in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update increases the production resource requests for the platform-gateway, raising CPU from 200m to 300m and memory from 200Mi to 320Mi. These adjustments are made in response to observed high CPU and memory usage on existing gateway pods, helping to alleviate throttling and scheduling pressures without changing the limits. Overall, this change aims to enhance the performance and reliability of the gateway service.

items.title: Disable GitHub proxy embeddings in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: Removed `text-embedding-3-small` and `text-embedding-ada-002` from the GitHub proxy checker configuration. The checker now prevents these embeddings from being re-added or re-enabled automatically. The live Mongo account was updated to immediately mark GitHub proxy embedding capabilities as disabled. This ensures embedding checks remain consistent with the updated configuration.

items.title: Enhance Python 3.8 Compatibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a fallback mechanism for parsing skill frontmatter in the AuthBackend, ensuring compatibility with Python 3.8. A safe try/except block wraps the frontmatter import, and a new fallback parser using PyYAML has been added. This change prevents crashlooping issues during initialization without altering existing API contracts or skill schemas.

items.title: Align Python SDK OpenAI Compatibility Mocks in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the Python SDK tests to ensure that the mock endpoints align with the actual OpenAI compatibility paths used by the SDK. The changes specifically update the test mocks in 'test_sdk.py' to replace outdated endpoint URLs, addressing recent CI failures caused by mismatched signatures. No runtime SDK code was altered, focusing solely on the test environment.

items.title: Enhance image generation with new parameters in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update syncs the SDK with the latest `nano-banana.json` OpenAPI spec by adding four crucial parameters to the `Images.generate()` method. The new parameters include `action`, `image_urls`, `aspect_ratio`, and `resolution`, enhancing the flexibility and functionality of image generation. Additionally, the documentation for `ace-token` and `claude-code` was removed as no SDK changes were necessary.

items.title: Enhance GitHub Proxy Model Mapping in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update maps GitHub proxy Claude model aliases to Copilot-native dot model IDs, ensuring compatibility with the upstream system. The changes maintain stability for public model names while correcting the underlying model references. Key models like Gemini, GPT, and embedding capabilities remain unchanged.

items.title: Sync Async Task Polling Docs with Latest OpenAPI in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the Skills documentation with the latest OpenAPI task-polling schema. Key changes include the replacement of `task_id` payloads with `id`/`ids` for both single and batch polling methods. Additionally, several skill-level endpoints have been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring consistency across the documentation.

items.title: Docs alignment verified for nano-banana in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update confirms the alignment of documentation for the `nano-banana` service with no code changes required. The endpoint and request shape parity checks were successful, ensuring that the service operates as specified. Other services, `ace-token` and `claude-code`, remain outside the scope of this sync.

items.title: Enhance Documentation Coverage in Docs 📚
items.summary: This update improves navigation coverage for the Claude Code, WebExtractor, and zh-Hans Publisher guide pages. It also consolidates NanoBanana MCP documentation by removing outdated files and ensuring the correct naming convention is maintained. Additionally, hidden guide pages that are no longer generated have been removed, streamlining the documentation for better accessibility.

items.title: Enhance GitHub Copilot Integration in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a Dockerized GitHub Copilot proxy service, enhancing the Platform Service's capabilities. Key changes include the addition of Kubernetes deployment configurations, a new checker configuration for OpenAI models, and automatic MongoDB account record updates. The integration aims to streamline interactions with GitHub Copilot, improving overall functionality.

items.title: Update to Official Logo Slices in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update replaces the hand-drawn default logo with official transparent logo slices for improved branding. The A mark is rendered as a PNG crop, while the `ceData` wordmark uses an alpha mask, adapting to light and dark modes seamlessly. Tenant-provided logos remain unaffected, ensuring a consistent user experience.

items.title: Sync Private Services and Regenerate OpenAPI in Docs 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that private services documented in the navigation or OpenAPI files are included when syncing from PlatformBackend. Additionally, the OpenAPI files for drawai, qrart, sora, tiktok, and tw have been regenerated to eliminate stale data. The excluded-service list remains unchanged, maintaining the integrity of the documentation.
